name: Four Grain English Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups of all-purpose flour, yeast, whole wheat flour, wheat germ, oats, dry milk, sugar, and salt. Mix well.
2. In a saucepan, heat water and oil until very warm (120-130°F). Add the heated liquid to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened.
3. Beat on medium speed for 3 minutes. Gradually stir in enough remaining all-purpose flour to form a firm dough.
4. Knead on a floured surface for 5 to 8 minutes. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour (30 minutes if using quick-rising yeast).
5. Punch down the dough. On a surface sprinkled with cornmeal, roll the dough to 1/2 inch thickness.
6. Cut into 3-inch circles using a biscuit or cookie cutter. Place the muffins on ungreased cookie sheets. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 30 minutes (15 minutes if using quick-rising yeast).
7. Bake on a lightly oiled electric griddle or frying pan at 325°F for about 8 minutes on each side until golden brown. Cool. Split and toast before serving.
